programmes.show_description: The members of legendary cult band Cardiacs pick an hour of music on the theme of life-affirming, death-accepting.
10/7. Alexis Korner presents an in-depth documentary of the Rolling Stones' 1975 tour of America, talking to organisers and promoters, as well as band members and fans.
Alexis Korner continues his series on influential guitarists with a profile of the Who's Pete Townshend. First broadcast in 1983.
There's an early Queen session from 1973, The Icicle Works in concert, and BBC sessions from Public Image Ltd and Sebastien Tellier!
Soundtrack the early hours with back-to-back music, plus exclusive BBC sessions.
Chris Hawkins fires up Thursday with the biggest tunes and future classics.
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Lauren's got the finest music to motivate your Thursday, with the best tunes old and new.
Chloe from Lime Garden talks about their new album 'Maybe Not Tonight', and we boil the Kettle of Metal.
Huw has handpicked music, old and new, plus we round up the gigs, shows and festivals that everybody is talking about in today's Gig Lisztomania.
Reviewing this week's new releases.
Start your weekend early with a guest mix from Shy One, a producer and DJ from London whose new album Mali is due out on 12th March via South London label Touching Bass.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
A deep dive into the mind of an artist.
Get your fix of all the best new releases in one handy playlist.
Matt Everitt previews the week's best brand new albums, as we hear from Joshua Idehen, Cobrah and Bonnie 'Prince' Billy.
6 Music takes you through the early hours, playing back-to-back music.
6 Music goes back to the 90s!
6 Music goes back to the 00s!
Chris celebrates International Women's Day with 30 minutes of remixed tracks from incredible women in music.
New music and the biggest Friday bangers with Chris Hawkins.
Rapper Denzel Curry chats to Nick about his new album and touring with Deftones.
Musician and spoken word performer Joshua Idehen joins around his debut album, I Know You're Hurting, Everyone is Hurting, Everyone is Trying, You Have Got to Try.
Craig spins some joyous tunes to get your weekend started, before giving YOU the chance to pick the last song of the show!
It's Friday, so Huw is out to prove that any song sounds better when it's played on 6 Music by Huw Stephens - so let us know what you want to hear.

programmes.show_description: Ease from the end of your working week with joyful global selections. A place where people come together through their shared love of music.
Nothing but solid gold upbeat indie bangers; a throwback to the indie tunes you love with a sprinkling of the finest future hits.
Ladytron takeover the Indie Forever playlist playing their favourite indie hits!
